The libgc source is distributed here as an amalgamation (https://sqlite.org/amalgamation.html).
This means that, rather than mirroring the entire bdwgc repo here, the amalgamate tool
was used to bundle all C files and local includes together into a single C file, which is
much easier to handle.  This helps keep the V source distribution small, can reduce compile
times by 3%-15%, and can help C compilers generate more optimized code.

For generating the libgc amalgamation, the following commands were used:

    git clone https://github.com/ivmai/bdwgc.git
    cd bdwgc
    ./autogen.sh
    ./configure --enable-threads=pthreads \
        --enable-static \
        --enable-shared=no \
        --enable-thread-local-alloc=yes \
        --enable-parallel-mark \
        --enable-single-obj-compilation \
        --enable-gc-debug

NOTE (cx fork, P0 / cx-private #14): the bundled amalgamation was originally
generated with `--enable-thread-local-alloc=no`. We flip it to `yes` so the
Linux / `-prod` gc.o path matches the prebuilt macOS libgc
(thirdparty/tcc/lib/libgc.a, the archive the macOS `cx` build links), which is
already built with TLA on. Rather than regenerate the whole amalgamation, the
flip is applied directly in gc.c by changing the autoheader config line
`/* #undef THREAD_LOCAL_ALLOC */` to `#define THREAD_LOCAL_ALLOC 1` (the TLA
source is already present in the amalgamation, guarded by `#ifdef
THREAD_LOCAL_ALLOC`). Parallel-mark stays enabled at compile time but is pinned
to a single marker at runtime on macOS via `GC_set_markers_count(1)` (emitted
before `GC_INIT()` by the V cgen main template, vlib/v/gen/c/cmain.v). Together
these ease MARK-bound multi-thread contention; neither removes the fundamental
`GC_allocate_ml` alloc-lock serialization, so alloc-heavy `[?map [par]]` still
anti-scales — see bench/parallel-alloc/README.md and P0-ACCEPTANCE.md.
        
    ../../../cmd/tools/amalgamate -o ../gc.c \
        -b atomic_ops.h \
        -b gc/gc.h \
        -b gc/gc_backptr.h \
        -b gc/gc_disclaim.h \
        -b gc/gc_gcj.h \
        -b gc/gc_inline.h \
        -b gc/gc_mark.h \
        -b gc/gc_pthread_redirects.h \
        -b gc/gc_tiny_fl.h \
        -b gc/gc_typed.h \
        -b gc/javaxfc.h \
        -b il/PCR_IL.h \
        -b mm/PCR_MM.h \
        -b psp2-support.h \
        -b stubinfo.h \
        -b th/PCR_ThCtl.h \
        -b vd/PCR_VD.h \
        -s include \
        -s include/private \
        extra/gc.c

The updated header files are then copied into the include/gc directory.  We can delete
include/gc/gc_cpp.h since this header is not needed by V.  And, we can remove the git
repo for bdwgc.

    cp include/gc/*.h ../include/gc
    cd ..
    rm include/gc/gc_cpp.h
    rm -rf bdwgc
